meta.title: Data Preparation Tips and Tricks
meta.description:
TIMESTAMPS
0:00 Welcome
3:00 Fine-web
12:14 Clustering and balancing data - Meta Paper
16:45 Clustering analysis in Colab
29:30 How to prepare chat / Q&A datasets synthetically
37:00 Q&A
37:20 Handling labeled data for fine-tuning
40:10 Setting a chat template for a tokenizer without one
42:00 Considerations on novel data and hallucinations
45:00 Issues with tokenizer and chat template not aligning
47:00 Using mixed-language datasets and their impact on training
49:00 Recommendations for models suitable for text classification
50:40 Extracting structured data from PDFs and tables
52:10 Multi-GPU training considerations
53:00 Using the LLM to VEC method for embeddings
53:40 Rag pipeline suggestions

In this video I run through considerations in building datasets:
1. Importance of filtering and deduplication - e.g. FineWeb: https://github.com/huggingface/datatrove/blob/main/examples/fineweb.py
2. Importance of balancing concepts/meaning - Meta paper on hierarchical k-means filtering: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2405.15613
3. I give a demo of balancing a dataset (OpenAssist - https://huggingface.co/datasets/Trelis/openassistant-llama-style)

➡️ More Resources and Support at Trelis.com/About

Notebook link: https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1yZVqTYCghHAyFtIPakopvWHQ8oN369u-?usp=sharing
